User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Viewora AI Coach application, including their economic and progression state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

auro_balance.description: User's current balance of Auro, the in-app currency.

level_name.description: User's current level name in the gamification system (e.g., Neuner, Vexer).

is_mentor.description: Flag that indicates if the user has reached the 'Vexer' level and gained mentor status.

weekly_free_refill_date.description: Timestamp tracking the last weekly free Auro refill.

completed_modules.description: An array of IDs for the academy modules the user has completed.

onboarded.description: Flag to check if the user has completed the onboarding process.

Photo.title: Photo
Photo.description: Represents a photo analyzed by the AI Coach.

id.description: Unique identifier for the photo entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the photo was analyzed.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a lesson in the Viewora Academy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lesson entity.

category.description: Category of the lesson (e.g., Composition, Lighting).

imageURL.description: URL of the image associated with the lesson.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a transaction (purchase, gift, or refill) of Auro.

id.description: Unique identifier for the transaction entity.

amount.description: Amount of Auro involved in the transaction.

transactionDate.description: Timestamp of when the transaction occured.

ChatMessage.title: Chat Message
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message in a chat room.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Accessible only by the user. Uses path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores photos analyzed by the AI Coach, associated with a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the photo.

definition.description: Stores lessons in the Viewora Academy. Publicly accessible.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for the lesson.

definition.description: Stores transaction records for each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

definition.description: Stores details about photography competitions. Publicly readable.

params.name: competitionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the competition.

definition.description: A collection of photos curated for public viewing (e.g., top-rated). Publicly readable.

params.name: photoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the photo.

definition.description: Stores messages within a specific chat room.

params.name: roomId
params.description: The unique ID for the chat room.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID for the message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Viewora AI Coach application's features, focusing on secure access, scalability, and maintainability. The key principle is Authorization Independence, achieved through denormalization. The structure is segregated to provide homogeneous security postures across collections.

*   **Users (Private Data):** User data is stored under `/users/{userId}` ensuring only the authenticated user can access their own data. Path-based ownership simplifies security rules.
*   **Photos (User-Owned Data):** Photos analyzed by the AI are stored under `/users/{userId}/photos/{photoId}`. This hierarchical structure maintains the 1:N relationship between Users and Photos. The `userId` is denormalized into the `Photo` document to enforce authorization independence.
*   **Lessons (Public Data):** Lessons are stored in the `/lessons/{lessonId}` collection.  These are globally accessible so no user-specific authorization is applied.
*   **Transactions (User-Owned Data):** Transaction data is stored under `/users/{userId}/transactions/{transactionId}`. This structure allows tracking of each user's transactions and follows the 1:N relationship between Users and Transactions. The `userId` is denormalized into the `Transaction` document to enforce authorization independence.
*   **Competitions & Public Photos (Public Data):** The `/competitions` and `/public_photos` collections store publicly accessible content. They are separated to allow global read access without compromising user data privacy.
*   **Chat Rooms (Semi-Private Data):** Chat messages are stored under `/chat_rooms/{roomId}/messages/{messageId}`. This allows signed-in users to participate in chats without being tied to a specific user's private data tree.

This design supports the required QAPs by:

1.  **Authorization Independence:** Denormalizing `userId` into the `photos` and `transactions` collections eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations.
2.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  The structure enables secure `list` operations. Listing photos is secure as all photos under `/users/{userId}/photos/{photoId}` belong to the authenticated user. Similarly, listing transactions is secure as all transactions under `/users/{userId}/transactions/{transactionId}` belong to the authenticated user. Public collections are safe to list.
3.  **Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The structure makes authorization intent explicit.  The use of path-based ownership for user-specific data clearly indicates that access is controlled by the user's ID.
